sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 19.86 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.19)

statement.latex: Suppose that a finite group$G$has a$_{i}$-Hall subgroup for every$i I$. Suppose that a subgroup$A G$is such that$A H$is a$_{i}$-Hall subgroup of$A$for every$i I$and every$_{i}$-Hall subgroup$H$of$G$(see 19.84 in Archive). Is it true that then$A$is${ }$-subnormal in$G$? An affirmative answer is known if$=\{\{2\}, \{3 \}, \}$and if$G$is$$-soluble.

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
